## [00:00 - 03:03]

Alright, let's jump into the SEO Action Plan. You have learned quite a bit about search engine optimization, but maybe you're wondering, hey, what do I do next? The SEO Action Plan takes everything that you've learned, specifically in the SEO Manual, and throughout all the resources and puts it in a step-by-step format. So the goal here is to help you create your strategy, optimize your website, and start ranking your pages on the search engines. Very first step is to make sure you set up your Google Search Console, and this is where you're going to do some basic checks. Just make sure your website's working. You'll be able to find out what keywords are ranking and just get some real visibility of what's going on with your SEO. Next you want to set up your Google Business Profile. This is going to allow you to, first of all, show up better for your business name, but also just really tell Google what you're all about. So fill out all the information that you can and be very active with your profile. And any sort of basic website optimizations that you need to do, we've got a good list here for you, but ultimately you just want to make sure that your website is performing as a whole because if you can't get your site performing, you can't get your individual pages to rank. And you'll see here as we go through this, this not only links to all of the resources in this program, but other resources that are valuable as well. So these are the same resources that are referenced in all the other materials, but we've just put it here in step-by-step format. Next is conducting your keyword research and choosing your phrases. Again, all of this is covered in great detail in the manual and in the training. Just really want to make it so you know what order of operation to go in, submit your website to any relevant directories. So there's going to be a lot of link building involved in SEO. And these are the links that, you know, rather than having to do these every day, these are the ones that you do once, you set up, and they're going to stay there forever. So get those out of the way and then really focus on creating your page optimization. Look at your individual pages, you know, figure out what's missing, do they need more content? How are they linked together within the page? So always look at all the resources provided. Obviously, we've given you a tutorial. There's a flow chart. There's checklists. And then as you'll see here, this is all about creating the content and the back links. So after you've done all the initial work, really what SEO comes down to on a day to day basis, other than optimizing new pages that you add. But once you've got your site and your pages optimized, it's all about creating new content and sending more links, quality links back to your site. Because every one of those links tells Google, hey, this is a site that you should be ranking. And then in the end, just regularly checking your ranking reports, whether you're just using Google Search Console, or you've decided to use a more professional tool like some of the ones that we recommend here. Just know that SEO is not a job that ever ends, but it is something that if you put
